name: Palestinian Arabic
notes: Palestinian Arabic ‚ÄĒ South Levantine, internally diverse along an urban/rural/Bedouin axis. INPUT CONTRACT: fully-diacritized Arabic. Ŕā qaf is the classic four-way split (Fadda 2016:28, after Al-Wer & Herin 2011:60): URBAN koine (Jerusalem, Jaffa, Nazareth) ‚Üí [ Ē]; CENTRAL/NORTHERN RURAL West Bank fellahin ‚Üí [k] (with the 'kaf shift' /k/‚Üí[t É]); SOUTHERN/Bedouin (Negev, and indigenous Gaza City) ‚Üí […°] (Cotter 2016:152); [q] retained only in qeltu enclaves. All four listed,  Ē first for the prestige urban form. ō¨ jim ‚Üí / í/ (urban) ~ /d í/ (rural/Bedouin). ŔÉ kaf ‚Üí [t É] before front vowels in central rural speech. Interdentals /őł √į √įň§/ merge to stops [t d dň§] in the urban koine (Cotter 2016:150-154, after Bergstr√§√üer 1915 & Palva 1984:361-62), conserved in rural/Bedouin varieties. Diphthong monophthongization /aj aw/‚Üí[eňź oňź] and emphasis backing are inherited from the Levantine parent.

sources.author: Cotter, W.M.
sources.title: One Piece of the Puzzle: Notes on the Historic Interdental Fricatives /őł, √į, √įň§/ in the Arabic Dialect of Gaza City
sources.publisher: Journal of Arabic and Islamic Studies 16
sources.notes: READ in full. Urban Palestinian interdentals ‚Üí stops [t d dň§] (Bergstr√§√üer 1915 Map 1; Palva 1984:361-62). Gaza City indigenous qaf ‚Üí […°] (Bedouin, Cotter 2016); historically [ Ē] (Bergstr√§√üer). Amman qaf variable […°]~[ Ē] (Al-Wer 2007:66-68, cited p.152).

sources.author: Fadda, H.
sources.title: Language Variation in Western Amman
sources.publisher: MA thesis, Canada Institute of Linguistics (CanIL)
sources.notes: READ (phonology chapter). Ammani koine consonants = urban Palestinian [ Ē t d dň§  í], 'identical to other Levantine cities' (pp.27-28). Qaf labels (Al-Wer & Herin 2011:60): [g] gilit, [q] qeltu, [ Ē] Jerusalem, [k] rural West Bank. Vowels: short /i u a/, long /iňź uňź eňź oňź aňź/ (Al-Wer 2011:4). Diphthongs /aj aw/‚Üí[eňź oňź] except before glide: bajt‚Üíbeňźt, sň§awt‚Üísň§oňźtň§ (p.30).

sources.author: Almbark, R. & Hellmuth, S.
sources.title: Acoustic analysis of the Syrian Arabic vowel system
sources.notes: READ in full. Damascus/Syrian vowel system: five long /iňź eňź aňź oňź uňź/, three short contrastive /i a u/; mid short [e o] and schwa are allophones of /i u/ (¬ß3-4). Mid-long /eňź oňź/ from /aj aw/ coalescence (¬ß1.1). /aňź/ higher/fronter in Coastal Syria than Damascus (p.2).

sources.author: Cowell, M.W.
sources.title: A Reference Grammar of Syrian Arabic (Based on the Dialect of Damascus)
sources.notes: Standard Damascus grammar. Primary text NOT obtained (Internet Archive borrow-only, no full text); its Damascene vowel classification (5 long / 5 short + schwa) is cited here only as reported and tested by Almbark & Hellmuth 2015 ¬ß1.3.
